journal: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement (IEEE TIM)
topic: Zero-shot fault diagnosis based on transformer-based methods

structure.notes: Both examples use 5-section structure (I-V). Section II covers background theory, Section III details the proposed method, Section IV presents experiments with multiple cases/datasets.

abstract.notes: Single paragraph, no line breaks. Include key contributions and accuracy numbers.

introduction.notes: 4-6 paragraphs covering: problem significance, existing methods and limitations, proposed solution, contributions list (3-4 items), paper organization.

background.notes: 2-4 subsections covering key theoretical foundations with equations.

method.notes: 3-5 subsections. Include model architecture, training procedure, evaluation criteria. Equations for loss functions and key formulations.

experiments.notes: Multiple cases/datasets. Include dataset description, experimental setup, results analysis, comparison with state-of-the-art, ablation studies.

conclusion.notes: Summarize contributions (numbered list), mention future work briefly.

references.intro_ratio: >=50% of total references must appear in Introduction

types: Results plots (accuracy curves, confusion matrices, t-SNE)
figures.notes: Key conceptual figures use figure* for double-column display. Data figures can be single-column.

equations.notes: Include loss functions, distance metrics, key mathematical formulations of the proposed method.

formatting.paper_organization: Last paragraph of Introduction describes remaining sections

quality_criteria.novelty: Must clearly state what is NEW compared to existing methods
quality_criteria.motivation: Problem significance must be established with real-world context
quality_criteria.comparison: Must compare with at least 4-5 state-of-the-art methods
quality_criteria.datasets: Validate on at least 2 benchmark datasets
quality_criteria.ablation: Include ablation study to validate each component
quality_criteria.visualization: Include t-SNE, confusion matrices, or similar visual analysis
